contentBlocks.original:
नमो॑ मि॒त्रस्य॒ वरु॑णस्य॒ चक्ष॑से म॒हो दे॒वाय॒ तदृ॒तं स॑पर्यत ।
दू॒रे॒दृशे॑ दे॒वजा॑ताय के॒तवे॑ दि॒वस्पु॒त्राय॒ सू॒र्या॑य शंसत ॥ 1
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namō̍ mi̠trasya̠ varu̍ṇasya̠ chakṣa̍sē ma̠hō dē̠vāya̠ tadṛ̠taṃ sa̍paryata ।
dū̠rē̠dṛśē̍ dē̠vajā̍tāya kē̠tavē̍ di̠vaspu̠trāya̠ sū̠ryā̍ya śaṃsata ॥ 1
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the all-seeing eye of Mitra and Varuṇa; worship that great deity — the Truth. Praise Sūrya, the far-seer, born of the gods, the bright banner, the son of heaven. (1)

contentBlocks.original:
सा मा॑ स॒त्योक्तिः॒ परि॑ पातु वि॒श्वतो॒ द्यावा॑ च॒ यत्र॑ त॒तन॒न्नहा॑नि च ।
विश्व॑म॒न्यन्नि वि॑शते॒ यदेज॑ति वि॒श्वाहापो॑ वि॒श्वाहोदे॑ति॒ सूर्यः॑ ॥ 2
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sā mā̍ sa̠tyōkti̠ḥ pari̍ pātu vi̠śvatō̠ dyāvā̍ cha̠ yatra̍ ta̠tana̠nnahā̍ni cha ।
viśva̍ma̠nyanni vi̍śatē̠ yadēja̍ti vi̠śvāhāpō̍ vi̠śvāhōdē̍ti̠ sūrya̍ḥ ॥ 2
contentBlocks.translation: May that Truth protect me on all sides, wherever heaven and earth are spread wide, wherever the days unfold. Everything that moves enters into something else; the waters flow everywhere, and everywhere Sūrya rises. (2)

contentBlocks.original:
न ते॒ अदे॑वः प्र॒दिवो॒ नि वा॑सते॒ यदे॑त॒शेभिः॑ पत॒रै र॑थ॒र्यसि॑ ।
प्रा॒चीन॑म॒न्यदनु॑ वर्तते॒ रज॒ उद॒न्येन॒ ज्याति॑षा यासि सूर्य ॥ 3
contentBlocks.transliteration:
na tē̠ adē̍vaḥ pra̠divō̠ ni vā̍satē̠ yadē̍ta̠śēbhi̍ḥ pata̠rai ra̍tha̠ryasi̍ ।
prā̠chīna̍ma̠nyadanu̍ vartatē̠ raja̠ uda̠nyēna̠ jyāti̍ṣā yāsi sūrya ॥ 3
contentBlocks.translation: No godless person abides near you, O Sun, as you drive across the sky with your fleet-winged horses. One realm follows after another; you travel onward, O Sūrya, with the light that shines through the waters. (3)

contentBlocks.original:
येन॑ सूर्य॒ ज्योति॑षा॒ बाध॑से॒ तमो॒ जग॑च्च॒ विश्व॑मुदि॒यर्​षि॑ भा॒नुना॑ ।
तेना॒स्मद्विश्वा॒मनि॑रा॒मना॑हुति॒मपामी॑वा॒मप॑ दु॒ष्ष्वप्न्यं॑ सुव ॥ 4
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yēna̍ sūrya̠ jyōti̍ṣā̠ bādha̍sē̠ tamō̠ jaga̍chcha̠ viśva̍mudi̠yar​ṣi̍ bhā̠nunā̍ ।
tēnā̠smadviśvā̠mani̍rā̠manā̍huti̠mapāmī̍vā̠mapa̍ du̠ṣṣvapnya̍ṃ suva ॥ 4
contentBlocks.translation: With that light, O Sūrya, by which you dispel the darkness and raise up the entire world with your radiance — with that very light, drive away from us all affliction, all disease, all evil dreams. (4)

contentBlocks.original:
विश्व॑स्य॒ हि प्रेषि॑तो॒ रक्ष॑सि व्र॒तमहे॑लयन्नु॒च्चर॑सि स्व॒धा अनु॑ ।
यद॒द्य त्वा॑ सूर्योप॒ब्रवा॑महै॒ त-न्नो॑ दे॒वा अनु॑ मंसीरत॒ क्रतु॑म् ॥ 5
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viśva̍sya̠ hi prēṣi̍tō̠ rakṣa̍si vra̠tamahē̍ḻayannu̠chchara̍si sva̠dhā anu̍ ।
yada̠dya tvā̍ sūryōpa̠bravā̍mahai̠ ta-nnō̍ dē̠vā anu̍ maṃsīrata̠ kratu̍m ॥ 5
contentBlocks.translation: Appointed guardian of the universe, you uphold the sacred law without giving offence as you rise of your own will. Whatever we implore of you today, O Sūrya — may the gods approve that intention of ours. (5)

contentBlocks.original:
त-न्नो॒ द्यावा॑पृथि॒वी तन्न॒ आप॒ इन्द्रः॑ शृण्वन्तु म॒रुतो॒ हवं॒-वँचः॑ ।
मा शूने॑ भूम॒ सूर्य॑स्य स॒न्दृशि॑ भ॒द्र-ञ्जीव॑न्तो जर॒णाम॑शीमहि ॥ 6
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ta-nnō̠ dyāvā̍pṛthi̠vī tanna̠ āpa̠ indra̍-śśṛṇvantu ma̠rutō̠ hava̠ṃ vacha̍ḥ ।
mā śūnē̍ bhūma̠ sūrya̍sya sa̠ndṛśi̍ bha̠dra-ñjīva̍ntō jara̠ṇāma̍śīmahi ॥ 6
contentBlocks.translation: May heaven and earth hear that prayer of ours; may the waters, may Indra, may the Maruts hear our call. May we not be deprived of the sight of Sūrya; may we, living long, reach a joyful old age. (6)

contentBlocks.original:
वि॒श्वाहा॑ त्वा सु॒मन॑स-स्सु॒चक्ष॑सः प्र॒जाव॑न्तो अनमी॒वा अना॑गसः ।
उ॒द्यन्त॑-न्त्वा मित्रमहो दि॒वेदि॑वे॒ ज्योग्जी॒वाः प्रति॑ पश्येम सूर्य ॥ 7
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vi̠śvāhā̍ tvā su̠mana̍sa-ssu̠chakṣa̍saḥ pra̠jāva̍ntō anamī̠vā anā̍gasaḥ ।
u̠dyanta̍-ntvā mitramahō di̠vēdi̍vē̠ jyōgjī̠vāḥ prati̍ paśyēma sūrya ॥ 7
contentBlocks.translation: Ever joyful, of clear sight, blessed with offspring, free from disease and sin — may we see you, O Sūrya, O great Mitra, as you rise each day; may we live long. (7)

contentBlocks.original:
महि॒ ज्योति॒र्बिभ्र॑त-न्त्वा विचक्षण॒ भास्व॑न्त॒-ञ्चक्षु॑षेचक्षुषे॒ मयः॑ ।
आ॒रोह॑न्त-म्बृह॒तः पाज॑स॒स्परि॑ व॒य-ञ्जी॒वाः प्रति॑ पश्येम सूर्य ॥ 8
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahi̠ jyōti̠rbibhra̍ta-ntvā vichakṣaṇa̠ bhāsva̍nta̠-ñchakṣu̍ṣēchakṣuṣē̠ maya̍ḥ ।
ā̠rōha̍nta-mbṛha̠taḥ pāja̍sa̠spari̍ va̠ya-ñjī̠vāḥ prati̍ paśyēma sūrya ॥ 8
contentBlocks.translation: Bearing great light, all-discerning, radiant, a boon for every eye to behold — ascending the vast expanse of heaven, may we, the living, behold you, O Sūrya. (8)

contentBlocks.original:
यस्य॑ ते॒ विश्वा॒ भुव॑नानि के॒तुना॒ प्र चेर॑ते॒ नि च॑ वि॒शन्ते॑ अ॒क्तुभिः॑ ।
अ॒ना॒गा॒स्त्वेन॑ हरिकेश सू॒र्याह्ना॑ह्ना नो॒ वस्य॑सावस्य॒सोदि॑हि ॥ 9
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yasya̍ tē̠ viśvā̠ bhuva̍nāni kē̠tunā̠ pra chēra̍tē̠ ni cha̍ vi̠śantē̍ a̠ktubhi̍ḥ ।
a̠nā̠gā̠stvēna̍ harikēśa sū̠ryāhnā̍hnā nō̠ vasya̍sāvasya̠sōdi̍hi ॥ 9
contentBlocks.translation: All beings advance and withdraw with the night at your signal, O golden-haired Sūrya. Being yourself without sin, grant us day by day ever greater well-being. (9)

contentBlocks.original:
श-न्नो॑ भव॒ चक्ष॑सा॒ श-न्नो॒ अह्ना॒ श-म्भा॒नुना॒ शं हि॒मा श-ङ्घृणेन॑ ।
यथा॒ शमध्व॒ञ्छमस॑द्दुरो॒णे तत्सू॑र्य॒ द्रवि॑ण-न्धेहि चि॒त्रम् ॥ 10
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śa-nnō̍ bhava̠ chakṣa̍sā̠ śa-nnō̠ ahnā̠ śa-mbhā̠nunā̠ śaṃ hi̠mā śa-ṅghṛṇēna̍ ।
yathā̠ śamadhva̠ñChamasa̍ddurō̠ṇē tatsū̍rya̠ dravi̍ṇa-ndhēhi chi̠tram ॥ 10
contentBlocks.translation: Be gracious to us with your sight; gracious with your day; gracious with your splendour; gracious with your warmth. As you have blessed the path and the household — so, O Sūrya, bestow upon us manifold wealth. (10)

contentBlocks.original:
अ॒स्माक॑-न्देवा उ॒भया॑य॒ जन्म॑ने॒ शर्म॑ यच्छत द्वि॒पदे॒ चतु॑ष्पदे ।
अ॒दत्पिब॑दू॒र्जय॑मान॒माशि॑त॒-न्तद॒स्मे शं-योँर॑र॒पो द॑धातन ॥ 11
contentBlocks.transliteration:
a̠smāka̍-ndēvā u̠bhayā̍ya̠ janma̍nē̠ śarma̍ yachChata dvi̠padē̠ chatu̍ṣpadē ।
a̠datpiba̍dū̠rjaya̍māna̠māśi̍ta̠-ntada̠smē śaṃ yōra̍ra̠pō da̍dhātana ॥ 11
contentBlocks.translation: O gods, bestow shelter on both our forms of birth — upon the two-footed and the four-footed. May they eat and drink and be nourished by strength; lay upon us that blessed freedom from harm. (11)

contentBlocks.original:
यद्वो॑ देवाश्चकृ॒म जि॒ह्वया॑ गु॒रु मन॑सो वा॒ प्रयु॑ती देव॒हेल॑नम् ।
अरा॑वा॒ यो नो॑ अ॒भि दु॑च्छुना॒यते॒ तस्मि॒न्तदेनो॑ वसवो॒ नि धे॑तन ॥ 12
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yadvō̍ dēvāśchakṛ̠ma ji̠hvayā̍ gu̠ru mana̍sō vā̠ prayu̍tī dēva̠hēḻa̍nam ।
arā̍vā̠ yō nō̍ a̠bhi du̍chChunā̠yatē̠ tasmi̠ntadēnō̍ vasavō̠ ni dhē̍tana ॥ 12
contentBlocks.translation: Whatever offence we may have committed, O gods, through word of tongue or through an impulse of the mind — whatever evil-doer harms us — lay that sin upon him, O Vasus. (12)
